bio: Hi, I'm Wahb — a self-taught full-stack developer who started coding just over a year ago and hasn't stopped shipping since. I build practical tools: web apps, backend services, and AI-powered systems. Most recently I've placed in two consecutive international hackathons — 3rd out of 775 participants at Hack for Humanity 2026 with EcoLens, and Technical Award winner at Hackonomics 2026 out of 1481 participants. Both solo builds. I care about making things that actually work — fast, reliable, and grounded in real-world needs.
quickFacts: Ships full projects end-to-end — from idea to deployed product
Integrates AI & computer vision into real, working apps
Manages own Linux servers and self-hosted infrastructure
Focused on fundamentals, clean architecture, and long-term growth

timeline.title: Early 2025
timeline.desc: Learned the basics of how websites are built and styled.

timeline.title: Spring 2025
timeline.desc: Started building small interactive projects and learning how logic and code work together.

timeline.title: Mid 2025
timeline.desc: Built complete web applications with modern tools and real features like dashboards and authentication.

timeline.title: Summer 2025
timeline.desc: Launched multiple real projects and ran them on my own Linux servers.

timeline.title: Late 2025
timeline.desc: Deepened focus on performance, Core Web Vitals, and production-grade code quality.

timeline.title: March 2026
timeline.desc: 🏆 Placed 3rd at Hack for Humanity 2026 — built EcoLens solo, an AI-powered waste classifier with gamified impact tracking, competing against 775 participants internationally.

timeline.title: April 2026
timeline.desc: 🏆 Won the Technical Award at Hackonomics 2026 — built EconoQuest solo, an economics simulation game with a microservices backend, competing against 1481 participants internationally.

timeline.title: Now
timeline.desc: Going deep on DSA, mathematics, and ML fundamentals — building the foundation for serious AI/ML research.

timeline.title: Next
timeline.desc: Building an end-to-end ML audio pipeline for speaker diarization and publishing original research.
